initiative_name: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 2. Direction and structure of measures
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
coverage_assessment: The sub-actions cover both pillars described in the parent: improving awareness/understanding (Action 1) and improving regional capacity to manage risks (Actions 2–6). Key impact areas mentioned (natural disasters/heavy rain, health/heat disorder, heat island, and monitoring/evaluation) are addressed, but the plan lacks explicit performance metrics (e.g., coverage of warning systems, greened area) and timelines to demonstrate comprehensive delivery.
coherence_assessment: The sub-actions are complementary: awareness-raising (Action 1) supports uptake; sectoral adaptation implementation (Actions 2–5) targets major local risks; and monitoring/evaluation capacity (Action 6) supports iterative improvement. There is no clear sequencing or deadlines, but there are also no contradictions or redundancies that undermine the package.
weak_signals: Promote/advance/consider language, no explicit deadlines/years, no quantified outcomes/coverage metrics, several process-only actions (publicity, adding an 'adaptation viewpoint', considering a system)
strong_signals: Concrete interventions named (disaster information sharing system, enhancement of delivery systems, city renovation for disaster prevention, creation of water and green network, roof/wall greening), monitoring/evaluation system concept with named partners (state and universities)
initiative_reasoning: 1) Objective: establish an adaptation program under two pillars (awareness/understanding; regional capacity to manage climate risks) and implement measures spanning disasters, health, urban heat, and learning/evaluation. 2) Sub-action strength mix: 0 strong, 2 moderate (Actions 3 and 5), 4 weak (Actions 1, 2, 4, 6) due to missing targets and timelines and, for several, primarily process/communications language. 3) Parent inheritance: the parent provides thematic structure (two pillars) but does not supply a binding mechanism (e.g., ordinance) or explicit spatial scope; thus weaker subs are not lifted into enforceable/measurable commitments. 4) Coverage/coherence: coverage is reasonably broad across key climate risks and enabling functions (score 0.65), and coherence is good because actions are complementary and non-conflicting (score 0.75), though sequencing and measurable milestones are absent. 5) Applying the initiative soundness rule: it cannot be SOUND because aggregate_measurability is 'No' and there is no 'strong' sub-action; however, it qualifies as PARTIAL because coverage_score >= 0.4 and at least one sub-action is moderate and the cluster contains concrete deliverables (e.g., disaster information systems, greening), even though no measurable targets/deadlines are stated. Therefore the initiative_result is PARTIAL, with a cautious confidence of 0.6 (also capped because > half of subs are weak).
